methods.description: Process for collecting and describing sediment cores using different coring techniques to preserve depositional features and prepare samples for further analysis.

steps.description: The corings were done with an Ejikelkamp hand auger using an Edelman head for sediment above the groundwater table and 1 m long gauge below the groundwater table.
steps.searchable_summary: use Ejikelkamp hand auger with Edelman head for sediment coring

steps.description: Samples collected through the Edelman head have a vertical length of 10 cm and a diameter of 7 cm and each sample could be mixed during coring operation.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples with Edelman head

steps.description: Gouge samples are generally almost undisturbed and, especially in silty and clayey deposits, internal depositional features like millimetric laminations could be preserved.
The depth of the samples was checked in the field and the uncertainty was ±0.05 m.
steps.searchable_summary: measure sample depth with ±0.05 m uncertainty

steps.description: The coordinates of the sampling sites and their elevation were measured in the WGS84 system using a Leica differential GPS with a vertical precision of ±0.02 m.
steps.searchable_summary: measure site coordinates and elevation with Leica GPS

steps.description: The elevation measures were corrected to the local geoid considering as a reference datum the top of the present salt marshes existing in Mirna Bay.
steps.searchable_summary: correct elevation measures to local geoid

steps.description: The cores were described in the field, but soon after they were protected with aluminium foil and sealed in plastic bags to preserve their characteristics for further lab analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: protect and seal cores for preservation

steps.description: The sediments were described following the method explained in FAO-ISRIC (2006): sediment colour and texture, primary sedimentary structures, and the type and concentration of accessory materials were the main features reported.
The colour of the layers was determined by comparing the moist sediment with the Munsell Soil Colour Charts.
steps.searchable_summary: determine sediment layer color using Munsell Charts

steps.description: The content of calcium carbonate was estimated observing the reaction with hydrochloric acid (10% of concentration) on a scale from 0 to 4.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate calcium carbonate content with HCl reaction

steps.description: The shells and their fragments were determined in the field and some have since been checked in the lab with a stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: identify shell fragments in field and lab
